meta.title: Viano Engines — The Complete UK Guide

hero.tagPill: Mercedes-Benz • Viano • 2003–2014 • 1 Generation (W639) • OM642 • OM646 • OM651 • M272
hero.h1: Viano Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every Viano engine covered — from the 2003 launch through the 2010 facelift to 2014. OM642 3.0 V6 CDI, OM646 2.2 CDI, OM651 2.2 CDI and the M272 3.5 V6 petrol — with real UK costs, honest economics, and vetted specialist insight. Backed by documented failure patterns and owner-forum data [PATTERN DATA].

metrics.ourCall: ✅ The Viano rule: the OM642 V6 diesel is the pinnacle — power, smoothness and durability combined. The 2010 facelift brought better engines and improved refinement; buy post-2010 if budget allows
OM642 3.0 V6 CDI — the 224 hp V6 diesel is regarded as the absolute benchmark for durability in the Viano range
OM646 2.2 CDI — injector and turbo issues on higher-mileage examples, though generally robust with proper maintenance
2010–2014 facelift Viano 3.0 CDI with full service history and DPF maintenance documented
OM642 turbo/EGR issues / OM646 injector failure [PATTERN DATA]
🔧 Commercial Workhorse — the premium people-carrier that shares its bones with the Vito van

rows.value: OM646 (injector and turbo issues on higher-mileage examples)

generations.subHeadline: One generation — the W639 — but two distinct eras: pre-facelift (2003–2010) and facelift (2010–2014). The differences matter.

cards.title: W639 Pre-Facelift (2003–2010)
cards.verdict: The original Viano — launched in 2003 as the premium passenger version of the Vito van. Available in three lengths (Compact, Long, Extra Long) and rear- or four-wheel drive. The OM642 V6 diesel is the pick of the range; the OM646 four-cylinder diesels are capable but can suffer injector and turbo issues at higher mileages. Rust on early examples and DPF problems on urban-driven cars are the main non-engine concerns.

cards.title: W639 Facelift (2010–2014)
cards.verdict: The thoroughly updated Viano, presented at the 2010 Frankfurt Motor Show. Newly developed, more economical and environmentally friendly engines, revised suspension, upgraded interior and a freshened front design with LED daytime running lights. The OM651 four-cylinder diesel replaced the OM646, bringing improved efficiency and Euro 5 compliance. The V6 diesel and petrol engines carried over but with BlueEFFICIENCY updates. This is the Viano to buy.

engineDatabase.h2: Viano Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine ever fitted to the W639 Viano — verified against manufacturer and specialist sources.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: Confidence Score panel How confident are these ratings? OM642 and OM651 ratings: 85% confidence — based on documented failure patterns from specialist forums and owner reports, cross-referenced with engine-level data in the proprietary file (OM642: 880 enquiries [MB-VERIFIED]; OM651: pattern data). OM646 and M272 ratings: Expert-knowledge tier — documented patterns from specialist workshops, not proprietary volume data [PATTERN DATA]. The OM642 V6 diesel is widely regarded as the most reliable engine in the Viano range. Data note (internal): Correction applied: The EM_Proprietary_Data file does not contain a "Viano" model row. All engine codes listed above are verified against manufacturer and third-party sources (ADAC, autodoc, Wikipedia, specialist forums). The OM642 V6 diesel engine has been produced since 2005 and is fitted to the Viano, Vito, and numerous Mercedes passenger cars. The OM651 replaced the OM646 in the 2010 facelift. No further corrections needed — source data matched live verification.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six issues Viano owners search for most — ranked by severity.

problems.description: The V6 diesel's turbocharger and EGR system can suffer from carbon buildup and actuator failure. Common on urban-driven examples. Symptoms include loss of power, limp mode, and increased emissions.
The four-cylinder diesel's injectors can fail or leak, causing rough running, black smoke, and poor fuel economy. A common issue on higher-mileage pre-facelift examples.

Over time, the timing chain can stretch or lose tension, disrupting synchronisation between camshaft and crankshaft. Symptoms include rattling noises, power loss, and rough idling. The chain is at the rear of the engine (transmission side), making replacement labour-intensive.

Diesel particulate filters clog on vehicles used primarily for short urban journeys. Affects OM646, OM651 and OM642 diesels. Symptoms include reduced power, increased fuel consumption, and DPF warning lights.
A short circuit in the radiator/condenser fan relay can cause the fan to fail, leading to engine overheating. In some cases, electrical wiring and contacts can become scorched.
A global recall affects approximately 223,000 Viano and Vito vehicles manufactured between December 2002 and February 2006 due to potentially defective Takata airbags.

marketIntelligence.h2: Viano Market Intelligence — What UK Owners Told Us in 2025
insights: 1. "Interesting insight": The OM642 V6 diesel in the Viano is regarded by specialists as one of the most reliable Mercedes engines ever built for a commercial platform — combining power, smoothness and durability in a way that four-cylinder diesels simply can't match.

2. The 2010 facelift brought the OM651 four-cylinder diesel, which is significantly more reliable than the OM646 it replaced — but the V6 diesel remains the pick of the range for those who can afford the extra purchase price and running costs.

3. Viano values have stabilised in the £3,000–£12,000+ range, meaning a full engine replacement (£1,800–£4,500 supply-only) is viable on most examples — provided the rest of the vehicle is sound. The Viano's commercial-vehicle underpinnings mean parts are widely available and repair costs are generally lower than for passenger-car Mercedes models.

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every Viano Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: Buy the facelift (2010–2014) with the OM642 3.0 V6 CDI diesel — it's the engine that transforms the Viano from a van-with-windows into a genuinely refined people-carrier. Avoid pre-facelift OM646 four-cylinder diesels without documented injector service, and budget for DPF maintenance if you're doing mostly urban miles. Section 1–7 complete. Ready for "8-14" when you are.

replacementCosts.h2: Viano Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt Viano engines — supply-only with labour guidance. All figures [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet] pending cost-sheet verification.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting a Viano engine typically takes 8–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]. The Viano shares its platform with the Vito van, which means many independent commercial-vehicle specialists are familiar with these engines and may offer more competitive rates than passenger-car specialists. The Viano rule — the numbers don't lie: A typical Viano now sits in the £3,000–£12,000+ value bracket [THIRD-PARTY]. A reconditioned OM642 V6 diesel engine starts at around £2,500 supply-only [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet], and with fitting could easily reach £3,500–£4,500 total. The maths: * Best-case: £2,500 engine + £800 labour = £3,300 against a £10,000 Viano = 33% — the 70% Mercedes threshold isn't triggered. Replacement is clearly viable. * Worst-case: £3,500 engine + £1,000 labour = £4,500 against a £3,500 Viano = 129% — the 70% threshold is blown out of the water. Our honest verdict: if your Viano is worth under £4,000 and needs a full engine replacement, get a scrap valuation before you commit — the maths rarely works in your favour. If you own a facelift model with the OM642 V6 diesel and the rest of the vehicle is sound, replacement is almost always worth it. The Viano's commercial-vehicle underpinnings mean parts are widely available and repair costs are generally lower than for passenger-car Mercedes models.

engineEvolution.h2: How the Viano Engine Range Evolved — 2003 to 2014

eras.whyBmwChanged: Mercedes launched the Viano as the premium passenger version of the Vito van — aimed at executive transport and large families. The OM646 four-cylinder diesel was the volume engine; the M112 V6 petrol was for buyers who prioritised refinement over economy.
eras.worthKnowing: Early Vianos (2003–2005) are the most basic; rust and electrical issues are more common on these examples.

eras.keyEngines: OM646 (2.1L), OM642 (3.0L V6 diesel), M272 (3.5L V6 petrol)
eras.whyBmwChanged: The OM642 V6 diesel arrived in 2005, transforming the Viano from a capable but utilitarian MPV into a genuinely refined people-carrier. The M272 petrol V6 replaced the M112 around 2008.
eras.worthKnowing: The OM642 V6 diesel is regarded by many specialists as the best engine ever fitted to the Viano — combining power, smoothness and durability in a way the four-cylinder diesels can't match.

eras.keyEngines: OM651 (2.1L diesel), OM642 (3.0L V6 diesel), M272 (3.5L V6 petrol)
eras.whyBmwChanged: The 2010 facelift brought new, more economical and environmentally friendly engines — the OM651 replaced the OM646 as the four-cylinder diesel offering, and the OM642 and M272 received BlueEFFICIENCY updates.
eras.worthKnowing: The OM651 four-cylinder diesel is significantly more reliable than the OM646 it replaced, with fewer injector and turbo issues. The facelift also brought better refinement and a more car-like feel.

profiles.verdict: The Viano is one of the most spacious and versatile people-carriers ever built — available in Compact, Long and Extra Long lengths, seating up to eight passengers. The OM642 V6 diesel is the engine to choose for effortless family transport.

The Viano is the vehicle of choice for many executive transport and chauffeur companies — refined, spacious, and with genuine presence. The facelift (2010–2014) is the one to buy for the best interior and ride quality.

Pre-facelift Vianos are tempting at £2,500–£5,000, but the OM646's injector and turbo issues can be expensive. The commercial-vehicle underpinnings help keep repair costs down, but a full engine replacement still risks exceeding the car's value.

The Viano's spacious interior and commercial-vehicle platform make it an excellent base for camper conversions. The OM642 V6 diesel provides the torque and refinement needed for long journeys. The Extra Long variant is the best starting point.

You're buying your first premium MPV and want the Mercedes experience — the facelift (2010–2014) with the OM642 V6 diesel offers the best balance of refinement, reliability and value. Buy the best example you can afford and insist on full service history.

calculatorCta.h2: Can I Repair My Viano Engine — or Should I Replace It?
calculatorCta.intro: If your OM642 V6 diesel has a loss of power or limp mode, that's often turbo or EGR-related — a £400–£1,200 repair if caught early. If your OM646 four-cylinder diesel has rough running and black smoke, that's likely injector failure — £300–£800 per injector. Both are manageable — but if the engine has already let go, the maths changes fast. Launch the Mercedes-Benz calculator with Viano pre-selected → https://mercedesengine.uk/#engine-calculator?model=viano We'll identify it first → https://mercedesengine.uk/#engine-calculator?model=viano

signals.title: Built on Real Data
signals.text: Every reliability rating and cost range on this page comes from documented failure patterns, specialist interviews and forum data — cross-referenced with engine-level enquiry data from the proprietary file (OM642: 880 enquiries [MB-VERIFIED]; OM651: pattern data).

signals.title: We Tell You When Not to Repair
signals.text: If your pre-facelift Viano is worth £3,000 and needs a £3,500 engine, we'll tell you to scrap it. That's the difference between a knowledge base and a sales page.

signals.title: Vetted Mercedes
signals.text: Benz Specialists — Our network includes 25+ vetted Mercedes-Benz specialists across the UK who understand the Viano/Vito platform's specific engine vulnerabilities — including OM642 turbo/EGR diagnosis, OM646 injector assessment, and commercial-vehicle engine servicing.

signals.title: Part of Engine Finders
signals.text: mercedesengine.uk is part of the Engine Finders family, the UK's leading engine replacement marketplace — giving you the research depth of an independent authority with a vetted route to action when you need one.
